perTask.note: core util, very common name -> grep-hostile (27 callers in-graph)

discrepancies.note: Axios.js:150 `utils.merge(headers.common, headers[config.method])` is lexically inside the `_request` method (lines 82+, before next member). Real call to the default-export merge via the `utils` default object. Both sets agree.

fetch.js:75 `utils.merge.call({skipUndefined:true}, {...}, env)` is inside the `factory` arrow (line 68). Real call. Manual found it; codeweb's --callers output OMITTED it (codeweb-missed-real relative to the raw --callers list), though --impact DID include fetch.js:factory. Net: REAL caller, present in confirmedTruth.

mergeConfig.js:39 `utils.merge.call({caseless}, target, source)` and :41 `utils.merge({}, source)` are both inside nested `function getMergedValue` (line 37). Innermost enclosing symbol = getMergedValue. Real. Both sets agree.

getMergedValue is nested inside the outer `export default function mergeConfig` (line 17), so mergeConfig is an enclosing (non-innermost) scope of the same two call sites. codeweb reports it via any-enclosing-scope attribution; it is a REAL transitive enclosure but a DUPLICATE of the getMergedValue site at innermost granularity. Not separately added to confirmedTruth to avoid double-counting one physical call; not a false positive.

merge recurses on itself at utils.js:477 `merge(existing, val)` and :479 `merge({}, val)`. The innermost enclosing symbol is the inner `assignValue` closure; the enclosing named function is `merge` itself. This is a REAL self/recursive reference. codeweb labels the closure `assignValue` (see next) but does not list `merge` itself; I record the canonical enclosing function symbol `lib/utils.js:merge` in confirmedTruth.

codeweb attributes the utils.js:477/479 recursive calls to the inner named arrow `const assignValue = (val, key) => {...}` (line 463), which is the true innermost enclosing symbol. This is CORRECT and more precise than my function-level attribution. Same physical recursive call site as lib/utils.js:merge above — one real reference, two valid enclosing-symbol labels (assignValue innermost, merge outer).

Module scope of utils.js references `merge` only at line 983 inside the default-export object literal (`merge,` shorthand). That is the DEFINITION/export of the symbol, not a call/dependency on it. Listing module-scope as a caller is an export-site artifact, not a genuine caller. Excluded from confirmedTruth.

Verified: toFormData.js contains ZERO occurrences of `merge` (grep -c = 0). It only does `import utils from '../utils.js'` and uses unrelated members (isPlainObject, isArray, etc.). It never reads utils.merge. codeweb's caller entry is a false positive — likely from coarse default-import edge attribution (any utils consumer counted as a caller of every utils member). Excluded from confirmedTruth.

axios.js L14 imports AxiosError and L60 assigns axios.AxiosError = AxiosError (module scope) -- a real re-export reference. codeweb --callers OMITS it (it is import-kind, not call-kind). codeweb's graph DOES contain the edge but mis-attributes it to lib/axios.js:createInstance; the actual reference is at module scope. --impact surfaces lib/axios.js:<module> and :createInstance.

class CanceledError extends AxiosError (L5, inheritance) + super(...,AxiosError.ERR_CANCELED,...) (L16) inside constructor. A real dependency. codeweb --callers OMITS it (kind=inherit, filtered out); graph has the inherit-edge and --impact lists it.
static from() at L77 does new AxiosError(...). Self-referential real caller. Both sets agree.

new AxiosError L24 inside assertValidHttpProtocolURL. Real. (buildFullPath only calls assertValidHttpProtocolURL, so it is NOT a direct AxiosError caller; --impact adds buildFullPath transitively, correctly excluded from --callers.)
new AxiosError L19 inside settle. Real.

AxiosError.from L133 is inside transformResponse (the second transform fn), NOT transformRequest. codeweb's graph edge mis-labels the enclosing symbol as transformRequest; the real enclosing symbol is transformResponse. The FILE is correctly identified by codeweb as a caller; only the symbol name is mis-attributed. Truth: transformResponse.
instanceof AxiosError L22 and new AxiosError L33, both lexically inside composeSignals. Real.

new AxiosError L11 inside throwIfDepthExceeded. Real. (codeweb graph also has an import-edge mis-attributed to formDataToJSON, which itself does not construct AxiosError; covered by throwIfDepthExceeded.)
new AxiosError at L35/58/67 inside fromDataURI. Real.

AxiosError.from L75 inside resolveConfig. codeweb --callers OMITS it (the edge is kind=import, filtered out, even though L75 is an actual call). --impact lists resolveConfig. A real caller missing from --callers output.
new AxiosError L142/152 inside convertValue. Real.
new AxiosError L160 inside throwIfMaxDepthExceeded. Real. (graph import-edge mis-attributed to toFormData is covered by these two call-callers.)
new AxiosError L42 inside the arrow returned by validators.transitional (a module-scope property assignment); codeweb maps to <module>. Real.
new AxiosError L83/96/104 inside assertOptions. Real.

index.js L8/L30 destructures AxiosError from the runtime axios object and re-exports it -- this is axios.AxiosError (a property access on the default instance), NOT a static binding to lib/core/AxiosError.js:AxiosError. It re-exports the same class at runtime but is not a statically-resolvable reference to the target file symbol. Found by manual sweep; excluded from confirmedTruth as a value re-export across the public-API boundary rather than a direct symbol reference. (Borderline; documented.)

discrepancies.site: index.d.ts: export class AxiosError / class CanceledError extends AxiosError / AxiosError: typeof AxiosError
discrepancies.note: index.d.ts L509/534/551/738 are TypeScript ambient TYPE declarations of a same-named class -- a same-named different thing, not a reference to the JS runtime class. codeweb --impact lists index.d.ts:<module> as downstream type surface, but it is not a caller. Excluded.

These import AxiosError from the 'axios' PACKAGE specifier (not the local file) and assert typeof AxiosError === 'function' / axios.AxiosError === AxiosError. They reference the class only at runtime through the external package's public surface, which is a different resolution root than lib/core/AxiosError.js. codeweb correctly has no local edge. Excluded from confirmedTruth as not a static reference to the target file symbol (real runtime references, but cross the package boundary).

Only a code COMMENT mentions AxiosError (L53: '...any AxiosError it throws...'); dispatchRequest.js does not import or reference the class. codeweb --callers correctly omits it. (--impact lists dispatchRequest symbols transitively, which is impact semantics, not a direct reference.)
isAxiosError() checks payload.isAxiosError === true (a duck-typed property), and does NOT import or reference the AxiosError class. Same-named property, different thing. Correctly excluded by both.
Only axios.isAxiosError(err) (the helper) at L53; no reference to the AxiosError class. Not a real reference. (Appeared in a substring count but is the isAxiosError helper.)

regression.test.js & instance.browser.test.js only compare against the STRING literal 'AxiosError' (error.name); api.test.js & errorDetails.test.js only use the isAxiosError helper. No class reference. Excluded.

discrepancies.note: Both found it. codeweb modeled it as a CALL edge (the only one besides the module self-ref). Code: new AxiosHeaders() (724), AxiosHeaders.from(config.headers).normalize() (735), new AxiosHeaders(res.headers) (1146) — all inside httpAdapter (spans 518-1413). Genuine instantiation + static-call caller.

codeweb's --callers includes the file's own module scope as a 'caller' (the module-level AxiosHeaders.accessor([...]) call at 329, utils.reduceDescriptors(AxiosHeaders.prototype,...) at 339, utils.freezeMethods(AxiosHeaders) at 349, and the class declaration itself). I excluded it from confirmedTruth as a self-reference (the defining module configuring its own class), not an external caller — but it IS a real same-file reference, so this is a defensible inclusion. Borderline; kept out of confirmedTruth on the 'external dependents' reading.

REAL caller --callers omitted. config.headers = AxiosHeaders.concat(contextHeaders, headers) at line 157 (inside _request, spans 82-233; class Axios spans 22-240). codeweb recorded an IMPORT edge attributed to Axios.js:Axios (the class), so call-only --callers/--impact miss it. The actual call site is the _request method.

REAL caller --callers omitted. const requestHeaders = AxiosHeaders.from(_config.headers).normalize() at line 20, inside the named module-default Promise executor dispatchXhrRequest (17-227). codeweb created no node for this anonymous adapter fn and modeled the file's usage as an IMPORT edge attributed to xhr.js:onloadend, so --callers misses both sites.

REAL caller --callers omitted (codeweb attributed the file's IMPORT edge here, but import edges are excluded from --callers/--impact). const responseHeaders = AxiosHeaders.from('getAllResponseHeaders' in request && request.getAllResponseHeaders()) at line 47, inside onloadend (42-77). Genuine static-call caller.

REAL caller --callers omitted. AxiosHeaders.from(response.headers) at lines 439 and 543, inside the async (config) => {...} returned by factory (68-614). codeweb recorded an IMPORT edge from fetch.js:factory; call-only --callers misses it.
REAL caller --callers omitted. const headers = AxiosHeaders.from(context.headers) at line 18 inside transformData (the module default export, 15-28). Modeled as IMPORT edge; excluded from --callers.

REAL caller --callers omitted. const headersToObject = (thing) => (thing instanceof AxiosHeaders ? { ...thing } : thing) at line 6 — a module-scope arrow used by mergeConfig's mergeMap.headers. instanceof is a genuine runtime dependency on the class. codeweb attributed the IMPORT edge to mergeConfig.js:mergeConfig (the consumer of headersToObject); the literal reference site is headersToObject.
REAL caller --callers omitted. config.headers = AxiosHeaders.from(config.headers) at line 37 inside dispatchRequest (34-89). codeweb recorded an IMPORT edge here; --callers excludes it.

REAL caller neither --callers nor codeweb's import attribution captured at this granularity. response.headers = AxiosHeaders.from(response.headers) at line 62, inside the onAdapterResolution callback (49-65). codeweb folded all dispatchRequest.js usage onto the dispatchRequest import edge, so this nested-callback call site is not separately represented.

REAL caller, same situation as onAdapterResolution. reason.response.headers = AxiosHeaders.from(reason.response.headers) at line 82, inside onAdapterRejection (66-87). Real static-call site not represented by codeweb's single import-edge attribution.

REAL caller --callers omitted AND codeweb misattributed. if (source instanceof AxiosHeaders) source = source.toJSON() at line 38, inside the nested visit arrow (33-70) of redactConfig (29-73). This is NOT in class AxiosError (75-170), but codeweb attributed the IMPORT edge to AxiosError.js:AxiosError (the class). Truth: redactConfig/visit. Both the call-only exclusion and the symbol attribution are off.

REAL caller --callers omitted. newConfig.headers = headers = AxiosHeaders.from(headers) at line 56 inside resolveConfig (39-117). Modeled as IMPORT edge; excluded from --callers. (resolveConfig is itself called by both xhr and fetch adapters, so this is a load-bearing dependency.)

REAL caller --callers omitted. axios.AxiosHeaders = AxiosHeaders at line 78 (module scope) publicly re-exports the class onto the default instance — the canonical way consumers reach axios.AxiosHeaders. codeweb attributed the IMPORT edge to lib/axios.js:createInstance (extractor folds module-level imports onto the first function node); the assignment is actually at module scope. Real reference either way; excluded from --callers because it is an import-kind edge.

NOT a codeweb edge at all — index.js has ZERO outgoing edges in the graph. index.js destructures AxiosHeaders off the local `axios` object (line 18) and re-exports it (line 40). This is a transitive value re-export of the runtime class (public ESM entry surface). I list it in manualCallers as a real reference to the value, but EXCLUDE it from confirmedTruth: it references axios.AxiosHeaders (the already-wired property on the instance), not the class node directly, and codeweb correctly draws no edge to lib/core/AxiosHeaders.js from it. Documented as a known edge-of-scope re-export.

TypeScript declaration files re-export the AxiosHeaders TYPE/class for the package's public type surface (export ... AxiosHeaders). These are type-level declarations consumed by tsc, not runtime callers of lib/core/AxiosHeaders.js. codeweb treats index.d.ts symbols as separate nodes with no edge to the runtime class. Correctly excluded from confirmedTruth by both methods.

discrepancies.note: Real references but classified as TEST edges by codeweb (8 test in-edges: axiosHeaders.test.js:<module>+HeaderBag, AxiosError.test.js:<module>+makeCircularCause, prototypePollution.test.js:<module>+startEcho, defaults.browser.test.js:<module>+MockXMLHttpRequest). Per query.mjs/graph-ops semantics, test edges are deliberately EXCLUDED from --callers (and from confirmedTruth as production callers). They would surface under --tests / --dependents. Excluded by design, not a miss.
